title: Past Tenses: Simple vs. Continuous
description: Revise the Past Simple and Past Continuous from Unit 1: Welcome to Ireland.

questions.text: Review: We use the Past Simple for a completed action in the past. We use the Past Continuous for an action that was in progress at a specific time or when another action interrupted it.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: While we were walking in Dublin, it started to rain.
While we walked in Dublin, it was starting to rain.
While we were walking in Dublin, it was starting to rain.
questions.hint: The continuous action is walking, and the interruption is the rain starting.

questions.question: Fill in the correct forms of the verbs (Past Simple or Past Continuous):
questions.text: Yesterday afternoon, while Anna [was reading|read|reads] (read) a book about the history of Ireland, she suddenly [heard] (hear) a loud crash. She [ran] (run) to the window and saw that a tree [had fallen] (fall) down. Some children [were playing|played] (play) near it just minutes before, but thankfully they [were] (be) safe.

questions.question: Choose the correct prepositions for time and place:

statements.text: We arrived [in] Dublin late at night.
He fell asleep [during] the history presentation.

questions.question: Rearrange the words to form a correct past tense sentence:
Write a short description of a holiday adventure (2-3 sentences). Use at least one verb in the Past Continuous (e.g., 'was walking', 'were visiting').
